results.query:
MATCH (_attack_pattern0:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT _attack_pattern0.name, _attack_pattern0.public_id, _attack_pattern0.description, _attack_pattern0.x_mitre_detection
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.comment: Using node and property name mapping: technique and id, name, description

results.query:
MATCH (_attack_pattern0:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT _attack_pattern0.name, _attack_pattern0.public_id, _attack_pattern0.description, _attack_pattern0.x_mitre_detection
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how campaign {c:campaign.name} ends up using technique {t.name}.

Answer: 
Campaign {c.name} is attributed to intrusion set {is:c.attributed-to.name}, which uses malware {m:is.uses>malware.name}, which uses attack-pattern {t:m.uses.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (c:campaign), (t:`attack-pattern`), (c:campaign)-[:`attributed-to`]->(is:`intrusion-set`), (is:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(m:malware), (m:malware)-[:uses]->(t:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT c.name, t.name, is.name, m.name
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how technique {t:technique.name} ({t.id}) is used both by tool {tl.name} and campaign {c.name} ({c.id}).
Answer: Technique {t} ({t.id}) is used by tool {tl:t.used-by>tool.name} and it is also used by malware {m:t.uses<malware.name} (ID {m.id}). Malware {m.id} is used directly by campaign {c:m.uses<campaign} (id {c.id}).
template_object.text-quick: technique {t.name}  {t.id} tool {tl.name} campaign {c.name} ({c.id}) technique {t} ({t.id}) tool {tl:t.used-by>tool.name} malware {m:t.uses<malware.name} (ID {m.id}) Malware {m.id} campaign {c:m.uses<campaign} (id {c.id}).

results.query:
MATCH (t:`attack-pattern`), (tl:tool), (c:campaign), (t:`attack-pattern`)<-[:uses]-(tl:tool), (t:`attack-pattern`)<-[:uses]-(m:malware), (m:malware), (m:malware)<-[:uses]-(c:campaign)

LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT t.name, t.public_id, tl.name, c.name, c.public_id, m.name, m.public_id
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text-long:
TODO: Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question:  
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
template_object.text: Instruction: ... Question: .... Answer: data component {dc:x-mitre-data-component.name} detects {t:dc.detects.name} detects {t2:dc.detects.name} spec version {t.spec_version[_='2.1']} t.platform_0 :{t.x_mitre_platforms[0]}  any t: {t.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']} all t2:{t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}

results.query:
MATCH (dc:`x-mitre-data-component`), (dc:`x-mitre-data-component`)-[:detects]->(t:`attack-pattern`), (dc:`x-mitre-data-component`)-[:detects]->(t2:`attack-pattern`), (t:`attack-pattern`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE elementId(t) <> elementId(t2) AND (t.spec_version="2.1") AND ("Windows" IN t.x_mitre_platforms) AND ALL(x IN t2.x_mitre_platforms WHERE x="Linux")
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT dc.name, t.name, t2.name, t.spec_version, t.x_mitre_platforms, t2.x_mitre_platforms
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text-long:
TODO: Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question:  
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
template_object.text: Instruction: ... Question: .... Answer: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} (on Windows: platforms={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique {t2:tl.uses.name} (on Linux: platforms={t2.x_mitre_platforms[?='Linux']}), both mitigated by mitigations {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (tl:tool), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t1:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t2:`attack-pattern`), (t2:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa1:`course-of-action`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa2:`course-of-action`)
WHERE elementId(t1) <> elementId(t2) AND ("Windows" IN t1.x_mitre_platforms) AND ("Linux" IN t2.x_mitre_platforms)
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, t1.name, t1.x_mitre_platforms, t2.name, t2.x_mitre_platforms, coa1.name, coa2.name
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.comment: Invisible Section: Filter for non-list property, #2
template_object.text-long:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question:  
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
template_object.text: Instruction: ... Question: .... Answer: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} <*(on Windows: platforms={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']})*> and technique {t2:tl.uses.name} <*(on Linux: platforms={t2.x_mitre_platforms[?='Linux']})*>, both mitigated by mitigations {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (tl:tool), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t1:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t2:`attack-pattern`), (t2:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa1:`course-of-action`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa2:`course-of-action`)
WHERE elementId(t1) <> elementId(t2) AND ("Windows" IN t1.x_mitre_platforms) AND ("Linux" IN t2.x_mitre_platforms)
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, t1.name, t1.x_mitre_platforms, t2.name, t2.x_mitre_platforms, coa1.name, coa2.name
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how tool {tl:tool.name} uses a technique that works on Windows and another that works only on Linux, both techniques having the same course of action.
Answer: Tool {tl.name} uses two different techniques, {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}. Both techniques are mitigated by mitigation {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name}<*INVISIBLE: {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name} constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}*>. Technique {t1.name} works on platforms (inclding Windows): {t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']} and technique t2 works only on platform <* ONLY LINUX*>{t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).
template_object.text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}. Constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}. t1 platforms(?Win)={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique t2 platform(*Linux) {t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).

results.query:
MATCH (tl:tool), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t1:`attack-pattern`), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t2:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa1:`course-of-action`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa2:`course-of-action`), (coa1:`course-of-action`), (coa2:`course-of-action`), (t1:`attack-pattern`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE coa1.public_id = coa2.public_id AND elementId(t1) <> elementId(t2) AND ("Windows" IN t1.x_mitre_platforms) AND ALL(x IN t2.x_mitre_platforms WHERE x="Linux")
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, t1.name, t2.name, coa1.name, coa2.name, coa1.public_id, coa2.public_id, t1.x_mitre_platforms, t2.x_mitre_platforms
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: How could course of action {coa1:course-of-action.name} (id {coa1.id}) prevent achieving tactic {ta1.name} (id {ta1.id})?
Answer: mitigation or tactic {coa1.name} mitigates attack pattern {ap1:coa1.mitigates.name}, which achieves tactic {ta1:ap1.achieves.name} (id {ta1.id}).
Modification times: 
coa1: {coa1.modified}
ap1: {ap1.modified}
ta1: {ta1.modified}. <*{coa1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]}  {ap1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} {ta1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} *>
template_object.text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}. Constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}. t1 platforms(?Win)={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique t2 platform(*Linux) {t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).

results.query:
MATCH (coa1:`course-of-action`), (ta1:`x-mitre-tactic`), (coa1:`course-of-action`)-[:mitigates]->(ap1:`attack-pattern`), (ap1:`attack-pattern`)-[:achieves]->(ta1:`x-mitre-tactic`), (ap1:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE (coa1.modified>=datetime('2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z')) AND (ap1.modified>=datetime('2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z')) AND (ta1.modified>=datetime('2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z'))
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT coa1.name, coa1.public_id, ta1.name, ta1.public_id, ap1.name, coa1.modified, ap1.modified, ta1.modified
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.comment: VERSIONING: used global constraint on modified datetime using generation config field. See below.
template_object.comment 2: 'apply_to': 'all' means that the given date constraint applies to ALL nodes involved, incuding intermediary
template_object.comment 3: 'apply_to': 'any' means that the given date constraint applies to AT LEAST one node involved in the query path solution, incuding intermediary nodoes

modified_constraints.comment: after, before, or none bay be omitted

template_object.order_by_ALT: other options: var.prop, var.prop, ..., random, none
